description: Bug Hunt mission generation - Compendium pp.184-190. 9-step mission setup procedure.
setup_steps: Place Signals, Tactical Locations, and initial Contacts

objectives.description: Place 6 dice (1-6) at significant locations. Roll 3D6 and remove non-matching dice. Remaining become Objectives.
objectives.placement_rule: Space evenly, avoid placing within 6" of battlefield edges.
objectives.vital_objective: If two of the 3D6 roll the same number, that Objective becomes Vital (counts as 2).
objectives.critical_objective: If all three 3D6 roll the same number, the one remaining Objective becomes Critical (counts as 3).

types.name: Patrol
types.achieved_when: At the end of each battle round, if a trooper is within 6", roll 1D6. On a 4+, the Objective is completed.

types.name: Scout
types.achieved_when: Completed at the end of the round during which a trooper moved to make contact with the marker.

types.name: Secure
types.achieved_when: Completed if at the end of a round, a trooper is within 6" and no enemies are within 6".

types.name: Retrieve
types.achieved_when: A trooper moves into contact and spends a Combat Action to pick up the package. If casualty, package drops. Must hold package when Evac'ing.

types.name: Beacon
types.achieved_when: Move a trooper into contact to complete, but only once all Hack, Secure, and Scout Objectives have been completed.

types.name: Hack
types.achieved_when: Trooper must move into contact and spend a Combat Action. Roll 1D6+Savvy, score 6+ to complete.

objectives.success_threshold: Two or more completed Objectives is considered a success for XP purposes.

difficulty_settings.name: Living Nightmare
difficulty_settings.special_rule: At end of each battle round, if fewer than 3 Contact markers on table, place 1 new Contact marker at center of random battlefield edge.

priority.description: Roll 2D6 and pick the lower result. This is your Priority Rating (1-6).
priority.reputation_spend: May spend up to 2 Reputation, each point raising or lowering Priority by 1.

loadout.description: Each main character may select Combat Rifle, Shotgun, or Boarding Sword for the mission.
loadout.hand_cannon_swap: One main character may swap their Service Pistol for a Hand Cannon for the mission.

initial_contacts.description: Place a Contact marker on each Objective. Place one additional in the center of the table.
initial_contacts.extra_for_difficulty: Add 1 extra center Contact for Mega-violence or Living Nightmare. Remove for I'm Too Pretty / Hey Not Too Crazy.

deployment.description: Randomly determine a battlefield edge. Set up anywhere within 3" of that edge.
deployment.objective_distance: Any Objectives must be at least 12" from initial deployments. Move them if closer.

evac.description: From Round 5 onwards, request Evac. Character must be stationary, not Stunned, cannot fire.
evac.savvy_bonus: If requesting character has Savvy +1 or greater, add +1
evac.arrival: Drop ship arrives at end of next battle round after success. Place marker anywhere. Troopers within 3" can evacuate.
evac.priority_increase: Each time a drop ship arrives, Priority increases by +1 for the remainder.
evac.supporting_fire: Drop ship has two LMGs, fires from center of marker with +1 Combat Skill. Cannot malfunction.

court_martial.description: If you failed to complete any Objectives, select the mission leader and roll D6. On a 1, they are removed from campaign.
court_martial.avoid_cost: Pay 3 Reputation to avoid this fate.

campaign_escalation_option.description: Optional: Use fixed Priority sequence instead of rolling.
